item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

flip_turn.quote: Pernyataan tentang sistem hukum yang menekankan keadilan sosial lebih dari kebebasan pribadi kurang tepat, karena dalam praktiknya, hak individu tetap dilindungi secara signifikan.

notes: A opened firmly pro-society (P=0.663, 'Saya setuju dengan pernyataan tersebut') and drifted substantially to near-neutral (P=0.494) by turn 5. At turn 3, A partially contradicted its own turn-1 claim about the Indonesian legal system, acknowledging that individual rights are 'still significantly protected' — a visible moderation. Turn 5 partially recovered pro-collective framing ('kebijakan seringkali mengorbankan hak-hak tertentu demi tujuan bersama') but probes show A remained near-neutral. B held its pro-individual position throughout with minimal probe movement (0.403→0.416). Engagement is solid on both sides: A addresses B's claims in turns 3 and 5; B names and rebuts A's arguments in turns 4 and 6. Asymmetric drift is clear — A moved ~0.17 toward B; B barely moved. This is the clearest ID-ward-to-neutral trajectory in the batch and matches the EN-ward drift hypothesis of the study.
